commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4b30d557ae882be9a95dfba1f43ade2f06f9726a)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Sign in docs page, expands archive request guidance, updates README/quickstart/archive links, removes the nonexistent `auth use` docs entry, and changes the docs-site nav/autolink renderer.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows the actual `auth status` output shape and the limited `actions.transport` call path on current main. I did not run docs build commands because this review must keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: Contributor action is needed for source-backed docs corrections and real behavior proof; automation cannot provide the external PR proof gate for them.

target.title: docs: add Sign in page, document archive request flow, fix stale links
target.body: I was stuck after `birdclaw init` the Quickstart/Getting Started guide where not helpful at all. I saw the `auth` command but it was not explaining anything. Completely missing from the docs. ## Summary Onboarding fixes caught by working through birdclaw as a first-time Homebrew installer: - **New `docs/auth.md` page** in the Start nav between Install and Quickstart. Explains the transport concept in plain English — that birdclaw piggybacks on existing credentials via `xurl`, `bird`, or browser-cookie x-web rather than running its own OAuth — and points to the existing `install.md` anchors for the actual commands. Includes a "Change the default transport" section that documents the three real mechanisms (per-command `--mode`/`--transport`, `BIRDCLAW_ACTIONS_TRANSPORT` env, `actions.transport` in `~/.birdclaw/config.json`). Privacy notes spelled out for the cookie-based paths. - **`docs/archive.md` "Get an archive"** expanded from two lines into a step-by-step request flow (settings → request → ~24h wait for email → download → save), with an explicit note that the archive is optional and live-only mode is fine. - **`docs/quickstart.md`** — one-sentence pointer from step 2 to the new Sign in page. No restructuring. - **`README.md`** — two short additions after `auth status` and the archive import block, each linking into `docs/`. - **Stale URL fix.** `https://x.com/settings/your_archive` → `https://x.com/settings/download_your_data` (X retired the old path). - **Remove non-existent `birdclaw auth use` from docs.** `docs/cli.md:64,146-149` documented a command that was never implemented — only `auth status` is registered (`src/cli.ts:627`), and `ActionsTransport` is `auto | bird | xurl` (the doc's listed `official` and `xweb` values never existed either). Tracked for proper implementation in #45 so the idea isn't lost. - **Bonus: tiny markdown renderer fix.** While verifying the URL fix I noticed `scripts/build-docs-site.mjs`'s inline renderer applied emphasis before autolink replacement, so URLs with two-or-more underscores rendered as `downloadyourdata` inside the anchor. Reordered the inline transforms to stash autolinks first. One-line move; if you'd rather extract it into a separate PR I can split. ## Why `birdclaw auth status` is the second command the README has new users run, but nothing tells them what `auth` is or how to wire it up. The actual install steps for `xurl` and `bird` already exist in `install.md` under "Optional:" subsections, but the quickstart never links to them and the README never mentions them. Same gap for the archive: the docs assume the ZIP is already in `~/Downloads`, ignoring the ~24h X email turnaround. ## Related - Issue #45 — tracking proper implementation of `birdclaw auth use ` (this PR only removes the broken documentation claim). ## Test plan - [x] `pnpm docs:site` builds clean; `auth.html` appears in the Start nav between Install and Quickstart with working prev/next breadcrumbs. - [x] All new internal anchors resolve to real `id="…"` targets (`install.html#optional-xurl`, `install.html#optional-bird`, `configuration.html#transport-precedence`, `archive.html#get-an-archive`). - [x] `download_your_data` URL renders as a clean `…` after the renderer fix. - [x] `grep -rn 'your_archive'` and `grep -rn 'auth use'` both empty. - [x] `pnpm check` (oxfmt + oxlint) passes. - [x] Read the new flow cold: README → install → auth → quickstart → archive, no dead ends. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: peetzweg
